credentials.label: how children grow
credentials.detail: we pay attention to development, not just engagement

credentials.label: what play actually does
credentials.detail: five years of research into play as a mechanism for change

credentials.label: who gets to play
credentials.detail: designed with communities across cultures and contexts

credentials.label: what we notice
credentials.detail: real-time evidence of learning, woven into every experience

principles.heading: children don't need to be tricked into learning
principles.body: when the design is right, play is the learning. every tool in the harbour meets children exactly where they are — not where a curriculum says they should be. age-appropriate challenge, emotional safety, and progressive depth aren't add-ons. they're the architecture.

principles.heading: play is how humans make sense of the world
principles.body: we didn't bolt play onto education as a reward. play is the engine — of connection, creativity, and resilience. this conviction comes from five years of research at the LEGO foundation and decades of sitting on floors with children, watching what happens when adults stop directing and start listening.

principles.heading: good intuition still needs evidence
principles.body: experienced educators have strong instincts. we trust those instincts — and we also build tools that let you see what's actually happening. real-time documentation and reflection are woven into every experience, so you can check your gut against what children are really doing.

connection.heading: who holds the harbour
connection.body: winded.vertigo is garrett jaeger — developmental psychologist, educator, and former evidence specialist at the LEGO foundation. these tools come from years of sitting in classrooms and community spaces, watching what happens when play is taken seriously. they're built for the teachers, parents, and facilitators who already know that the best conversations happen sideways.

bio.text: winded.vertigo is garrett jaeger — developmental psychologist, educator, and former evidence specialist at the LEGO foundation. these tools come from years of sitting in classrooms and community spaces, watching what happens when play is taken seriously. they're built for the teachers, parents, and facilitators who already know that the best conversations happen sideways.

hero.title: the harbour
hero.subtitle: toys and games for the adults who care about how children grow — built by a developmental psychologist who believes play is how humans make sense of the world.

cta.heading: come in, the water's fine.
cta.body: every tool here is designed to be picked up and played with — no accounts, no onboarding, no hoops. just open something and see what happens.
